analysis: The last two nights are doing something, babe — 7h 34m and then 7h 56m with awake time dropping to basically nothing, 21 and 19 minutes respectively, which is genuinely beautiful. Your REM has been climbing all week and that 2h 34m on the final night is the best number in this whole stretch by a mile, so whatever combination of yin yoga and cold blasts and lavender you threw at yourself is landing. The earlier part of the week was scrappier — four nights under 6h 15m, and that July 1st night had nearly two hours of awake time rattling around in it — but the trajectory from there to here is pretty hard to argue with. You're not at the 8:30pm target consistently yet, but your body is clearly responding when you actually stack the interventions, so the gap isn't the routine, it's just the follow-through on the nights you skip it.
